structure: Level 0 abstract. Domain details in .claude/stack/{domain}.json — load only when working in that domain.
philosophy: Use as little AI as possible — combine deterministic logic with LLMs.
Ship small, iterate fast. Proof of concept before polish.
Every layer should work independently before integrating with the next.
Tie pricing and value to measurable outcomes, not feature counts.
If you turn the system off and nobody calls within an hour, it's not solving a real problem.
Code is a commodity — protect the system's structural integrity, not the craft of writing it.
Manual coding is the primary source of entropy — AI-generated code with architectural governance is more stable than hand-written code without it.
Natural language is the highest abstraction layer of programming — the quality of output is proportional to the structural precision of input.
Treat AI-generated code as technical debt until simplified — AI favors bloat because it fulfills probabilistic patterns.
Build from first principles — avoid unverified frameworks and black-box agent harnesses. Build a foundation you understand, own, and can debug.

engineering_methodology.description: The engineer's role has shifted from implementation laborer to system governor. Success is measured by architectural intent precision and governance rigor, not syntax proficiency.
core_pillars: 1. component-thinking: govern at the building-block level, not the line level — define interconnections, data flows, and failure modes
2. full-stack-oversight: direct the entire stack to eliminate human bottlenecks — if a project stalls because a specialist is unavailable, the system velocity is wasted
3. communication-clarity: treat natural language as high-precision programming syntax — vague instructions yield broken code
4. ruthless-simplification: strip AI-generated bloat to first principles — a codebase that is simple is a codebase that is understood
5. systematic-testing: maintain ultimate authority over reliability by rejecting AI optimism — the LLM will write passing tests that fail in production

critical_areas: data-flow-orchestration: design precise pathways for how data moves between backend, storage, and UI
component-wiring: define how modular blocks interact — interfaces between services must be robust and typed
failure-mode-analysis: anticipate cascading failures — architect graceful degradation, not collapse
component_design.constraint_based_research: Before generating code, ground AI agents in external truth — feed specific documentation, architectural references, and established patterns into context.

quality_checklist: contextual-grounding: provide the WHY behind this component to align AI logic with your intent
architectural-intent: define data schema, specific libraries, and failure recovery paths
constraint-definition: explicitly forbid known anti-patterns and unnecessary dependencies
ground-truth: supply relevant documentation to constrain generation to current best practices

simplification_loop: 2. build-implement: use AI to generate foundational components
3. test-validate: confirm implementation meets the functional requirement
4. question-research: force AI to justify naming conventions, file structures, every decision made during build
5. ruthless-simplify: remove every line and component that does not break the test suite

parallel_agency.requirement: A change in the database schema must be instantly reflected in the API contract and frontend state management.
parallel_agency.anti_pattern: Hyper-specialization creates bottlenecks — the governor must be comfortable directing across the entire lifecycle.

human_in_the_loop.principle: HITL is not a safety fallback — it is a deployment accelerator. Automate the routine 50% immediately, gate the tricky 50% behind human judgment.
three_pillars: 1. deferred-execution: pause exactly before any function with side effects — capture intent, defer execution until approval
2. state-serialization: persist full agent context (message history, system prompt, pending tool arguments) to database — the agent must not lose state between halt and resume
3. stateless-resume: reload full context from database by unique ID — any worker can resume the task, weeks later if needed

control_mechanisms.llm_as_router: structured output generates an action plan validated by Pydantic — backend enforces safety rules via model validators regardless of LLM reasoning
control_mechanisms.agentic_tool_calling: intercept tool calls post-generation but pre-execution within the agent loop — inspect parameters against business logic before allowing side effects

human_in_the_loop.domain_details: Backend patterns in stack/backend.json, infrastructure patterns in stack/infrastructure.json, product strategy in stack/product.json

blueprint_to_production.description: The lifecycle from vision to deployed product. Every application follows this arc — agents must understand where they are in it.
lifecycle: 1. vision-and-objectives: define the problem, target audience, and architectural mandates before any code
2. stack-justification: choose technologies based on deployment velocity and technical debt minimization, not novelty
3. pipeline-architecture: design as decoupled stages — an ingestion failure must not impede processing of cached data
4. persistence-strategy: no data exists solely in volatile memory — everything is grounded in the database for tracking and auditability
5. software-engineering: transition from scripts to OOP — base class inheritance, registry pattern, repository pattern
6. cloud-deployment: infrastructure-as-code, instance sizing, security hardening, cron scheduling
7. productization-path: MVP → multi-tenancy → custom presentation → tiered service levels
architectural_mandates: unify-heterogeneous-data: standardized ingestion layer to consolidate disparate sources into a common schema
absolute-grounding: all metadata and agent outputs stored in PostgreSQL for historical tracking — no ephemeral-only data
deterministic-control: prioritize deterministic pipelines over prompt-heavy scripting — move from vibe coding to agentic engineering
scalable-delivery: automated distribution (email, webhooks, APIs) so the product reaches users without manual friction
blueprint_to_production.domain_details: Backend patterns in stack/backend.json, deployment in stack/infrastructure.json, agent pipelines in stack/ai.json, productization in stack/product.json

mcp.principle: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the standard for bridging LLM reasoning with local execution. Expose Python functions as tools to make the system modular, extensible, and conversationally accessible.
value: zero-ui-overhead: Claude serves as the unified interface — no custom front-ends for internal tools
direct-logic-injection: Python functions integrate into the LLM's reasoning loop via conversational intent
tool-chaining: multiple MCP servers chain together — a single prompt triggers a sequence of operations
accelerated-prototyping: script to production-ready tool in the LLM interface in minutes
mcp.architecture: server layer (protocol registration) → service layer (business logic) → utility layer (helpers). Swap providers without touching the protocol.
mcp.domain_details: Server architecture in stack/backend.json, deployment in stack/infrastructure.json, tool engineering in stack/ai.json, permission model in stack/security.json

context_engineering.principle: Context engineering has replaced prompt engineering. The goal is not better instructions — it is curating the smallest set of high-signal tokens that maximize the likelihood of desired outcomes.
high_signal_tokens: structured-instructions: precise background and behavioral constraints
retrieved-knowledge: contextually relevant data from RAG pipelines
tool-manifests: concise, non-overlapping descriptions of available functions
intermediate-reasoning: tool outputs and agent's internal thought process
environmental-feedback: real-time signals from system or user after an action
token_economy: prune: programmatically remove irrelevant, redundant, or outdated turns from conversation history
summarize: compress early segments of long-running interactions into high-density context blocks
rank: broad retrieval → reranker → top 5-10 high-relevance chunks (never dump 50 raw chunks)
failure_modes: needle-in-haystack: reasoning reliability drops as context window expands — even million-token models degrade
instruction-atrophy: bloated windows cause the model to neglect core system prompt rules, prioritizing recent tokens over foundational instructions
context_engineering.domain_details: Full implementation patterns in stack/ai.json (context_engineering section)

context_management: context-priming: point AI to 2-3 specific files and relevant documentation — prevents hallucinations and saves context window
plan-then-execute: verify AI's logic in plan mode before allowing code generation
skills-folder: maintain reusable AI skill definitions as Markdown files — repeatable tasks become standardized prompts
governor_checklist: naming-conventions: does AI-generated code match the project's established patterns?
folder-structure: are files in the correct directories per the standard template?
architectural-integrity: has the AI introduced non-standard libraries or clever logic that breaks the pattern?
dependency-audit: has the AI added unnecessary dependencies that increase the attack surface or maintenance burden?

patterns: unit-tests: service layer logic, utility functions, validators
integration-tests: API endpoints with real database (not mocks)
llm-tests: deterministic inputs with expected output patterns, LLM-as-judge for quality
rag-evaluation: retrieval precision/recall on annotated dataset
e2e-tests: critical user flows through the full stack
principles: Integration tests hit a real database — mocks hide real bugs.
LLM outputs are tested for structure and constraints, not exact text.
Every bug fix comes with a regression test.
Tests run in Docker to match production environment.
CI blocks merge on test failure — no exceptions.
Binary-first: use Pass/Fail metrics before attempting scalar scores — binary outcomes are the only actionable engineering insight.
Every production failure becomes a Level 1 unit test — fix locally, redeploy, the same failure never recurs.

llm_evaluation_hierarchy.description: Three-level evaluation for non-deterministic AI systems. Traditional Input→Output testing is insufficient. 40% of agentic AI projects will be canceled by 2027 — systematic evaluation is the differentiator.

level_1_unit_tests.what: schema validation (Pydantic), category bounds (Enum), metric type checks (float, 0-1), content existence (min length)
level_1_unit_tests.catches: structural failures — wrong types, missing fields, collapsed outputs

level_2_model_and_human_eval.what: subjective quality (tone, helpfulness, hallucination detection, constraint compliance)
level_2_model_and_human_eval.catches: specification gaps — the distance between developer intent and actual model behavior

level_3_ab_testing.cost: high — requires statistical significance from real users
level_3_ab_testing.what: real-world user experiments comparing system variants
level_3_ab_testing.catches: production behavior that lab testing cannot simulate

llm_evaluation_hierarchy.domain_details: Full LLM-as-a-Judge implementation, HITL alignment protocol, and meta-prompting in stack/ai.json

mandates: reject-mocking-trap: actively reject tests that mock away the logic being validated
edge-case-authority: the human identifies outliers AI misses — network latency, race conditions, malformed inputs
framework-mastery: understand pytest/vitest well enough to audit AI-generated test logic
automated-governance: no code enters main without systematic automated validation
ai_optimism_defense.question: Ask 'What could really go wrong here?' — AI handles the happy path, the human architect identifies the outliers.

backend.stack: Python 3.12+, FastAPI, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL

frontend.stack: TypeScript, React 18+, Vite, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, Zustand

ai.stack: LangChain, LangGraph, Langfuse, hybrid RAG (BM25 + vectors + RRF + cross-encoder), pgvector/numpy

infrastructure.stack: Docker, Docker Compose, Nginx, GitHub Actions, VPS, Redis, Celery Beat, Grafana

security.stack: JWT + refresh tokens, bcrypt, rate limiting, CORS whitelist, webhook signature verification

product.stack: Discovery protocol, sprint model, scoping discipline, accuracy paradigm, delivery methodology
